title: CycleAlign: Iterative Distillation from Black-box LLM to White-box Models for Better Human Alignment
abstract: CycleAlign is a framework for aligning parameter-visible white-box models with parameter-invisible black-box models through iterative interactions. It uses in-context learning as a pivot to break bottlenecks in black-box models, employs agreement ranking as pseudo labels to dynamically update in-context demonstrations, and achieves state-of-the-art performance in alignment with human values in a low-resource way.

methodology.approach: Iterative distillation between white-box and black-box models
process: White-box model generates multiple responses for a given instruction
Black-box model ranks these responses using human-craft ranking prompt and static in-context demonstrations
Ranking signal optimizes the white-box model
Generated probability of responses from white-box model is considered a ranking judgment
Combining judgments from both models to extract consistent rank as pseudo labels

existing_limitations: RLHF has complex architecture and unstable training
Ranking-based methods require extensively annotated data
Unidirectional distillation from LLMs is limited by inherent bottlenecks

results.achievements: State-of-the-art performance in alignment with human values
